gate_0_13_close_condition: The spine is a secure edge-storage runtime bottleneck claim, and each borrowed OS/storage mechanism is classified as implemented analogue, measured comparison, baseline boundary, rejected non-claim, or scoped placement.

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q uses an append/replay pattern for its FUSE sidecar format; it does not claim an F2FS segment cleaner, NAT/SIT design, or kernel-integrated flash filesystem.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: Do not call the epoch log an F2FS implementation or a flash-translation/filesystem replacement.

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q borrows the idea of operation-shaped metadata records, but keeps a FUSE sidecar runtime with scoped parallel commit; it does not claim ScaleFS decentralization, kernel shared-memory metadata, or POSIX scalability.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: No ScaleFS-equivalent scalability or decentralized metadata claim is allowed.
required_mechanisms.prior_mechanism: Operation logs and decentralized filesystem metadata

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q has compact sidecar records and selective/windowed freshness tradeoffs, not an ext4 FastCommit path or a complete journaling filesystem redesign.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: Do not imply ext4 FastCommit semantics, broad selective flushing safety, or physical power-loss certification.
required_mechanisms.prior_mechanism: Compact metadata logging and selective flushing

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q can claim a scoped epoch-group mechanism only for the measured mounted path; it cannot claim broad multicore filesystem scalability without the concurrency-contract closeout.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: No broad scalability, multicore, or concurrent-client claim before Gate 0.15/A3 close.

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q may claim storage-visible classification and observability tradeoffs; it may not claim kernel QoS is generally impossible or always worse.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: No SQLite uniqueness or general kernel-QoS impossibility claim.
required_mechanisms.prior_mechanism: Kernel QoS and I/O scheduling controls

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q is positioned against kernel-native alternatives through measured or blocked rows, not by replacing their maturity or kernel trust boundary.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: No apples-to-apples fscrypt/dm-crypt speedup without matched measured rows.

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q may claim replay-after-advance fail-closed behavior under the retained model; it may not claim full rollback resistance, sealed-key release, or persistent PCR-bound freshness.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: No TPM rollback-resistance or PCR-bound freshness claim before Gate C6 closes.

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q keeps ordinary FUSE semantics in the verified path; eBPF/io_uring completion bypass is not a contribution.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: No eBPF/io_uring bypass claim unless production mounted-path evidence exists.
required_mechanisms.prior_mechanism: FUSE passthrough, eBPF, io_uring, and kernel-bypass completion
required_mechanisms.prior_source: Modern kernel bypass and tracing paths

required_mechanisms.contribution_boundary: AEGIS-Q may claim measured placement asymmetry and optional GPU maintenance work; it may not claim direct storage DMA, GPUDirect, dma-buf zero-copy, or foreground AI QoS recovery.
required_mechanisms.nonclaim_boundary: No NVIDIA mechanism is a paper mechanism without production mounted-path benefit evidence.
